input.prompt:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

data.content:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?
data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-05-14T01:08:29.096Z</current_datetime>

We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

input.initialPrompt:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

data.reasoningText:
The user has migrated from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform and is encountering exit code 8 when test assemblies produce zero tests. This is a known behavioral difference - Microsoft.Testing.Platform returns a non-zero exit code (8) when no tests are discovered, whereas VSTest treated this as a success.

Let me look at their test project to understand the current setup and provide the fix.

data.content:
Microsoft.Testing.Platform returns **exit code 8** when zero tests are discovered — unlike VSTest which returned 0. Since your tests are conditionally compiled out, no tests exist in the assembly on some configurations.

The fix is to set the `TestingPlatformShowTestsFailure` property to `false` — but actually the correct property for this specific issue is simpler: add `<ZeroTestsIsNotAnError>true</ZeroTestsIsNotAnError>` to your project (available in MSTest 3.8+).

arguments.new_str:
<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Prevent exit code 8 when conditional compilation excludes all tests -->
    <ZeroTestsIsNotAnError>true</ZeroTestsIsNotAnError>

<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Prevent exit code 8 when conditional compilation excludes all tests -->
    <ZeroTestsIsNotAnError>true</ZeroTestsIsNotAnError>

toolCalls.name: edit
toolCalls.args:
{"path": "/tmp/sv-474afd06fda640f490bb26202ddad90c/TestProject.csproj", "old_str": "    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>", "new_str": "    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Prevent exit code 8 when conditional compilation excludes all tests -->
    <ZeroTestsIsNotAnError>true</ZeroTestsIsNotAnError>"}

toolArgs.new_str:
<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Prevent exit code 8 when conditional compilation excludes all tests -->
    <ZeroTestsIsNotAnError>true</ZeroTestsIsNotAnError>

data.content: The `ZeroTestsIsNotAnError` property didn't work with this SDK version. Let me use the correct Microsoft.Testing.Platform approach — passing `--minimum-expected-tests 0` to the test runner:

arguments.old_str:
<!-- Prevent exit code 8 when conditional compilation excludes all tests -->
    <ZeroTestsIsNotAnError>true</ZeroTestsIsNotAnError>
arguments.new_str:
<!-- Prevent non-zero exit code when conditional compilation excludes all tests -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--minimum-expected-tests 0</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>

data.reasoningText:
The `ZeroTestsIsNotAnError` property didn't work - it still fails. Let me check what the correct approach is. The issue is that with Microsoft.Testing.Platform, zero tests causes a non-zero exit code.

Let me look at what other options are available. The property might be different or need to be passed as a command-line argument. Let me check the Microsoft.Testing.Platform documentation.

Actually, looking at the error more carefully - it says "Tests failed" even though it shows "Failed: 0, Passed: 0". The exit code from `dotnet test` is 1 (not 8 directly - dotnet test wraps it).
 
I think the solution is to use the `--minimum-expected-tests 0` command-line argument when running the tests, or configure it through a runsettings file. The MSBuild property approach might not be the right way to handle this with the newer Microsoft.Testing.Platform. I'll try the command line approach instead of looking for a dedicated property.

arguments.old_str:
<!-- Prevent exit code 8 when conditional compilation excludes all tests -->
    <ZeroTestsIsNotAnError>true</ZeroTestsIsNotAnError>
arguments.new_str:
<!-- Prevent non-zero exit code when conditional compilation excludes all tests -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--minimum-expected-tests 0</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>

toolCalls.name: bash

data.content: The log reveals `--minimum-expected-tests` doesn't accept 0. But there's a `--ignore-exit-code` option that maps exit code 8 (no tests ran) to 0. Let me use that:

arguments.old_str:
<!-- Prevent non-zero exit code when conditional compilation excludes all tests -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--minimum-expected-tests 0</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>
arguments.new_str:
<!-- Treat zero-tests-discovered (exit code 8) as success, matching VSTest behavior -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>

data.reasoningText:
The error says `--minimum-expected-tests` expects a "single non-zero positive integer value". So you can't set it to 0. But I can see from the help output that there's a `--ignore-exit-code` option:

```
--ignore-exit-code
    Do not report non successful exit value for specific exit codes
    (e.g. '--ignore-exit-code 8;9' ignore exit code 8 and 9 and will return 0 in these case)
```

This is exactly what we need! Exit code 8 is the "minimum expected tests not met" code. Let me use `--ignore-exit-code 8` instead.

arguments.old_str:
<!-- Prevent non-zero exit code when conditional compilation excludes all tests -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--minimum-expected-tests 0</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>
arguments.new_str:
<!-- Treat zero-tests-discovered (exit code 8) as success, matching VSTest behavior -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>

data.content:
**Fixed.** The issue is that Microsoft.Testing.Platform returns exit code 8 when no tests are discovered, unlike VSTest which returned 0.

The fix is adding this property to your `.csproj`:

```xml
<TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>
```

This tells the test runner to map exit code 8 (no tests executed) back to 0, restoring VSTest's behavior for assemblies that conditionally compile out all tests.
